items.name: Cloud Carbon Footprint
items.description: An open source tool that provides visibility and tooling to measure, monitor, and reduce your cloud carbon emissions.

items.name: Azure Emission Impact Dashboard
items.description: Power BI App that provides emissions for azure cloud resource usage.

items.name: Azure Cloud for Sustainability API
items.description: Microsoft Cloud for Sustainability provides APIs to access emissions data related to your Azure and Microsoft 365 usage.

items.name: Google Carbon Footprint
items.description: The Carbon Footprint dashboard displays estimated greenhouse gas emissions associated with the usage of covered Google Cloud services for the selected billing account.

items.name: AWS Customer Carbon Footprint Tool
items.description: The AWS Customer Carbon Footprint Tool provides estimates of the carbon emissions associated with AWS products and services. Data is updated with a delay of three months.

items.name: Li10 for AWS
items.description: Measures the emissions for AWS resources with Software Carbon Intensity methodology defined by the Green Software Foundation.

items.name: Boavizta Cloud-Scanner for AWS
items.description: Boavizta Cloud-Scanner calculates environmental impacts of your cloud compute and storage instances. It combines real time inventory and usage data from your cloud account with Boavizta API to offer a view of your impacts on a given region.

items.name: Green Cost Explorer for AWS
items.description: The Green Cost Explorer is a tool by The Green Web Foundation that helps visualize AWS cloud spending alongside carbon emissions data to support more sustainable cloud infrastructure decisions.

items.name: Scaleway Environment Footprint Calculator
items.description: Scaleway's Environmental footprint calculator allows you to measure your environmental impact to your use of Scaleway cloud and bare metal services.

items.name: Kepler
items.description: Kepler (Kubernetes-based Efficient Power Level Exporter) uses eBPF to probe performance counters and other system stats, use ML models to estimate workload energy consumption based on these stats, and exports them as Prometheus metrics.

items.name: KEIT
items.description: KEIT (Kubernetes Emissions Insights Tool) supplements the energy recording with Kepler with current grid carbon intensity values and embodied carbon emissions.

items.name: aether
items.description: A open telemetry exporter to calculate the carbon emissions generated by your cloud infrastructure.

items.name: OpenTelemetry Java Agent Extension (RETIT)
items.description: An extension for the OpenTelemetry Java Agent that collects resource demand data and publishes carbon emissions.

items.name: Dynatrace Carbon Impact
items.description: A proprietary solution that provides insights into the carbon footprint of cloud applications and infrastructure, helping organizations reduce their environmental impact.

items.name: InfluxData Telegraf
items.description: A plugin-driven server agent for collecting and reporting metrics, including energy consumption and carbon emissions.

items.name: Green Metrics Tool
items.description: The Green Metrics Tool is a developer tool indented for measuring the energy and CO2 consumption of software through a software life cycle analysis (SLCA).

items.name: GreenFrame
items.description: Estimates the carbon footprint of a user scenario on a web application. Full-stack analysis (browser, screen, network, server). Can be used standalone, in a CI/CD pipeline, and in conjunction with the greenframe.io service.

items.name: Cardamon
items.description: Cardamon is a tool to help development teams measure the power consumption and carbon emissions of their software. The tool is built around the concept of observations and scenarios.

items.name: LIMo
items.description: A lightweight monitoring tool for calculating the energy consumption of microservices in Docker containers.

items.name: Impact Framework
items.description: A framework for modeling and analyzing the environmental impact of software systems.

items.name: Android Power Profiler
items.description: A tool for profiling the power consumption of Android applications.

items.name: Greenspector Studio
items.description: A proprietary solution for measuring the energy efficiency and carbon footprint of websites and mobile applications on real devices on the test bench of Greenspector.

items.name: PowDroid
items.description: An open-source tool for measuring the power consumption of Android applications.

items.name: Android Runner
items.description: Android Runner (AR) is a tool for automatically executing measurement-based experiments on native and web apps running on Android devices.

items.name: JoularJX
items.description: JoularJX is a Java-based agent for software power monitoring at the source code level.

items.name: jPowerMonitor
items.description: JUnit Extension and Java Agent for energy consumption measurement.

items.name: DotNet-Counters
items.description: A performance monitoring tool for .NET applications that includes energy consumption metrics.

items.name: PyRAPL
items.description: A Python toolkit for measuring the energy consumption of Python code using Intel's Running Average Power Limit (RAPL).

items.name: CodeCarbon
items.description: A Python package for estimating and tracking the carbon emissions of compute-intensive code.

items.name: Tracarbon
items.description: Tracarbon is a Python library that tracks your device's energy consumption and calculates your carbon emissions.

items.name: oaklean
items.description: A tool for analyzing and optimizing the energy efficiency of Node.js applications.

items.name: Coppers
items.description: Coppers is a test harness for Rust that can measure the evolution of power consumptions of a Rust program between different versions with the help of Intel RAPL.

items.name: AI Power Meter
items.description: The library enables to monitor energy usage of machine learning programs. It uses RAPL for the CPU and nvidia-smi for the GPU.

items.name: Carbontracker
items.description: A tool for tracking and predicting the energy consumption and carbon footprint of training deep learning models.

items.name: ZEUS
items.description: Zeus is a library for measuring the energy consumption of Deep Learning workloads and optimizing their energy consumption.

items.name: ML CO2 Impact
items.description: A tool for estimating the carbon footprint of machine learning computations.

items.name: Green Algorithms Calculator
items.description: A tool for estimating the carbon footprint of computational tasks, including AI Model Training and inference.

items.name: Green Coding AI
items.description: This project aims to show how much asking an GPT uses in resources.

items.name: EcoLogits
items.description: EcoLogits tracks the energy consumption and environmental impacts of using generative AI models through APIs.

items.name: Electricity Maps
items.description: An API that provides real-time carbon intensity data for electricity grids around the world.

items.name: WattTime
items.description: An API that provides real-time and forecast marginal carbon emissions data for electricity grids.

items.name: Energy-Charts
items.description: A service that provides data on electricity generation, consumption, and carbon intensity for various countries in Europe.

items.name: Carbon-Intensity UK
items.description: An API that provides carbon intensity data for the UK electricity grid.

items.name: BoaviztAPI
items.description: An open source API that provides environmental impact data for IT equipment, including carbon footprint, energy consumption, and resource depletion.

items.name: Climatiq
items.description: A proprietary API that provides carbon emissions data for various activities, including cloud computing, to help organizations measure and reduce their carbon footprint.

items.name: ResilioDB
items.description: ResilioDB breaks down the electronic components of a device like RAM or CPU, in order to assess the environmental impacts of any possible configuration.

items.name: CarbonDB
items.description: CarbonDB collects and consolidates energy usage data from multiple sources within a company, providing a unified view of energy consumption. It accurately converts energy usage into carbon emissions, enabling precise tracking of a company's carbon footprint.

items.name: fruggr
items.description: A platform for managing IT inventory and analyzing its environmental impact.

items.name: Power Hog
items.description: A tool for monitoring the power consumption of desktop applications.

items.name: Watt Wiser
items.description: A desktop software energy consumption estimation tool built as part of the [Watt-Wise Game Jam](https://wattwise.games/). Usable for simple energy estimation and benchmarking on Linux, macOS, and Windows.

items.name: powerletrics
items.description: PowerLetrics is an open-source framework designed to monitor and analyze power consumption metrics at the process level on Linux. Similar to how top displays memory or CPU time, PowerLetrics provides the "Energy Footprint" of each process in real-time.

items.name: Powerstat
items.description: Powerstat measures the power consumption of a machine using the battery stats or the Intel RAPL interface. The output is like vmstat but also shows power consumption statistics.

items.name: Perf
items.description: A performance analysis tool for Linux that can also be used to measure energy consumption by using RAPL.

items.name: Turbostat
items.description: A tool for reporting processor topology, frequency, idle power-state statistics, temperature and power on X86 processors.

items.name: NVIDIA SMI
items.description: A tool for monitoring and managing NVIDIA GPUs, including power consumption.

items.name: Intel PCM
items.description: A tool for monitoring performance and energy metrics for Intel processors.

items.name: PowerTOP
items.description: A tool for diagnosing issues with power consumption and power management on Linux.

items.name: Scaphandre
items.description: A monitoring agent dedicated to energy consumption metrics of bare metal hosts and qemu/kvm virtual machines.

items.name: Cloud Energy
items.description: Cloud Energy is an XGBoost & linear model based on the energy data from the SPECPower database for the cloud to estimate wattage consumption of server by just a few input variables.

items.name: CPU Energy Meter
items.description: CPU Energy Meter is a Linux tool that allows to monitor power consumption of Intel CPUs at fine time granularities.

items.name: Eco CI
items.description: Eco CI is a project aimed at estimating energy consumption in continuous integration (CI) environments. It provides functionality to calculate the energy consumption of CI jobs based on the power consumption characteristics of the underlying hardware.

items.name: CO2.js
items.description: A JavaScript library for estimating the carbon emissions of websites and web applications.

items.name: Website Carbon Calculator
items.description: Website Carbon Calculator estimates the carbon emissions of a website by analyzing factors like data transfer size and energy source of the hosting provider. A badge is provided that can be added to the footer of websites.

items.name: Ecograder
items.description: Ecograder analyzes websites for environmental impact by evaluating performance, design, development practices, and hosting, offering insights and recommendations to improve digital sustainability. A badge is provided that can be added to the footer of websites.

items.name: EcoIndex
items.description: EcoIndex evaluates the environmental impact of web pages by analyzing technical performance, complexity, and resource usage, providing a sustainability score along with actionable recommendations for optimization.

items.name: Ecometer
items.description: Ecometer is a tool that assesses the environmental impact of websites by analyzing their technical structure, performance, and hosting, offering a clear eco-score and suggestions to reduce digital carbon emissions.

items.name: Kastor
items.description: Kastor estimates the carbon emissions of a website and analyzes it according to eco design and accessibility guidelines.

items.name: GreenIT-Analysis
items.description: A browser extension for analyzing the websites for best practices.

items.name: Firefox Power Profiler
items.description: A tool for profiling the power consumption of websites in Firefox.

items.name: Safari Web Inspector
items.description: A tool for inspecting and profiling websites in Safari, including energy impact.

items.name: IBM Turbonomic
items.description: A platform for optimizing cloud resources to reduce costs and carbon emissions.

items.name: Greenpixie GPX Data
items.description: A solution for optimizing cloud infrastructure to reduce carbon emissions.

items.name: txture
items.description: A platform for analyzing and optimizing cloud infrastructure for cost and sustainability.

items.name: OxygenIT
items.description: A solution for optimizing IT infrastructure to reduce energy consumption and carbon emissions.

items.name: Cycloid
items.description: A platform for managing and optimizing cloud infrastructure with sustainability in mind.

items.name: Elastic Universal Profiling
items.description: A profiling solution that helps identify energy-intensive code paths and optimize application performance and energy efficiency.

items.name: OpenTelemetry eBPF Profiler
items.description: A whole-system, cross-language profiler for Linux via eBPF.

items.name: KEDA
items.description: A Kubernetes-based event-driven autoscaler that can scale applications based on event metrics from various sources.

items.name: Goldilocks
items.description: A tool for identifying and setting resource requests and limits for Kubernetes workloads.

items.name: Karpenter
items.description: A Kubernetes node autoscaler that helps improve application availability and cluster efficiency.

items.name: kube-green
items.description: A Kubernetes operator to reduce CO2 footprint by shutting down idle resources.

items.name: DailyClean
items.description: Shutting down pods by cronjob.

items.name: Carbon Aware KEDA Operator
items.description: A Kubernetes operator that scales workloads based on carbon intensity data.

items.name: CLEVER
items.description: Container Level Energy-efficient VPA Recommender for Kubernetes

items.name: Carbon Aware SDK
items.description: A software development kit for building carbon-aware applications that can shift workloads to times and regions with cleaner electricity.

items.name: grid-intensity-go
items.description: A command-line tool, go library and Prometheus exporter for accessing carbon intensity data from various sources.

items.name: Camunda Carbon Reductor
items.description: A tool for optimizing business processes to reduce carbon emissions. It supports Camunda 7 and Camunda 8.

items.name: Carbon-Aware-Computing.com
items.description: An API service and tool suite for carbon-aware computing that uses data from Energy-Charts.

items.name: Grid-aware Websites
items.description: A library for making websites carbon-aware by adapting to the carbon intensity of the electricity grid.

items.name: Creedengo
items.description: A static code analysis tool for identifying energy inefficiencies in code.

items.name: CAST
items.description: A proprietary software intelligence platform that includes analysis of energy efficiency and carbon footprint.

items.name: ec0lint
items.description: ec0lint is a static code analysis tool that provides users with hints on how to reduce the carbon footprint of their websites during the development process.

items.name: aDoctor
items.description: A tool for detecting energy code smells in Android applications.
